name: extract ip
IP Access guard banner
check ping result
IP gate
node-red SERVER pings nano ESP32
info:
**Setting Arduino nano esp32 IP address**
- Open exec node 'ping ESP32'
- set nano esp32 IP address

Note: node 'check ping result' output is purely 
for debugging purposes

Note: the ping does not specifically look for the nano esp32: if ANY device occupies this
IP address, the ping will be successful.But because the nano esp32 has a static IP address,
assigned deliberately, this is not a real concern and this ping check is fairly robust.

name: fetch own (browser) IP to display in access guard banner: runs client side
info: Extract own (browser) ip address to display in access guard banner

name: fetch socket IP (runs server side)
info:
The socket IP is the client IP as seen by the server (this check runs at the server side).
Normally (no proxy, ...) it will be identical to the client IP (as in the client-side IP check).

The result of this check is then 'AND'-ed with the result of the 'ping ESP32' check.

Controls are enabled or disabled based on the result.
